prompt_inputs.goal: Muscle building through strength training with high protein intake

solution_criteria: Meal plan covers all meals for one full day (breakfast, lunch, dinner, and snacks)
Total daily calories support muscle-building goals for an athlete of this size (approximately 3000-3500 calories)
Protein content is high enough to support muscle growth (minimum 180-200g protein across all meals)
Meals are practical and concise with clear portions and macronutrient breakdown
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete
scenario: Testing with a strength-training athlete requiring high protein intake and heavy calorie needs for muscle building

prompt_inputs.goal: Carbohydrate loading and sustained energy for a 90-minute long run training session with proper pre/during/post-workout nutrition

solution_criteria: Meal plan covers one full day with breakfast, lunch, dinner, and snacks strategically timed around the long run
Emphasizes high-carbohydrate foods (at least 60% of calories) with appropriate timing before, during, and after the 90-minute training session
All meals and snacks respect the vegetarian and nut-free dietary restrictions
Plan is compact and concise (under 400 tokens)
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete
scenario: Testing with an endurance runner needing carbohydrate loading and sustained energy release before/during/after a long training session

prompt_inputs.goal: Maintain weight at 71 kg for lightweight boxing competition while fueling high-intensity training sessions

solution_criteria: Meal plan totals approximately 2,400-2,600 calories to maintain current weight
Macros include 140-160g protein, 65-75g fat, and 300-350g carbohydrates to support training
All meals are vegetarian and dairy-free
Plan is compact and fits within one day with 3-4 meals/snacks
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete
scenario: Testing with a weight-class athlete (boxer, wrestler) needing to meet strict calorie/macro targets while staying within competition weight limits
